title: Amendment 2 Forward Viability Check: ANIMA for Stage 7

content.question: Can ANIMA's W⊕I⊕T architecture satisfy Stage 7 (self-representation of update rule as modifiable data)?
content.stage7_definition: The system can represent and modify its own update rule as first-class data. Exit criterion: the system produces a modified update rule that passes the ground truth test and outperforms the original. The modification is not a trivial reparameterization.
content.living_seed_failure: Living Seed failed Amendment 2 because its core equation phi[k] = tanh(alpha*x + beta*(x[k+1]+gamma*s)*(x[k-1]+gamma*s)) is Python source code — not data that the system can read, modify, and execute. No self-representation mechanism exists or can be added within that substrate.
content.anima_architecture_summary: ANIMA implements S=(V,tau,F,phi) with V={W,I,T}: W (World State, tau=S) updated from sensory evidence only; I (Internal State, tau=M) updated from prediction error; T (Temporal State, tau=D) advancing from activity cycles. Update rules are parameterized by PyTorch nn.Linear and nn.Sequential weight tensors, not hardcoded scalar equations.
content.current_anima_status: ANIMA does NOT currently satisfy Stage 7. W, I, T weights are initialized at construction and updated only by external backprop (if training) or not at all (at inference). The update rule weights are not accessible to I's dynamics as first-class modifiable data during operation.
content.key_structural_difference_from_living_se: The Living Seed's equation is source code — category error prevents self-representation. ANIMA's update rules are weight tensors — they ARE data, stored as FloatTensors in memory. This is not Stage 7 compliance yet, but it removes the categorical impossibility: the substrate CAN in principle read its own weights as tensors.

stage7_mechanism_path.name: Hypernetwork I layer (meta-learning within W⊕I⊕T)
stage7_mechanism_path.description: Add a hypernetwork sublayer H to I that: (1) reads I's current internal state as input; (2) outputs weight deltas or full weight matrices for I's own update_gate, error_gate, candidate layers; (3) applies those weights to compute the next I update. This implements 'I modifies its own update rule via I's own dynamics' — the exact requirement of Stage 7.
stage7_mechanism_path.principle_ii_compliance: The signal driving weight modification is I's internal state — a byproduct of the same dynamics that process input. No external evaluator required. Principle II satisfied.
stage7_mechanism_path.principle_iii_compliance: After each self-modification event, the modified I is tested against the pre-modification I. Comparison runs on novel inputs not seen during the modification step.
stage7_mechanism_path.ground_truth_compatibility: Stage 7's exit criterion (modified rule passes ground truth AND outperforms original) is architecture-independent. ANIMA can run the ground truth test (distinguishable persistent outputs) on both versions.
stage7_mechanism_path.W_enables_what_Living_Seed_lacked: W can carry global coupling information (the architectural wall c007 hit in the Living Seed). I conditioned on W can produce context-dependent weight modifications — this resolves the local-only wall that blocked beta/gamma. W is the lateral signal source that makes I's self-modification informed rather than local-only.

what_must_still_be_designed: The hypernetwork H architecture (its own layer structure — frozen at construction time)
The interface between I state and H input
The modification rule: delta-weights vs full weight replacement vs low-rank updates
Stability bounds: without damping, self-referential weight updates can explode (c008 analog)
what_becomes_adaptive: I's update_gate, error_gate, candidate weight matrices become I's own output
The equation form of I's update is no longer static Python code but dynamically generated tensors
W-conditioned weight modification enables topology-like routing effects within I
remaining_frozen_frame_analysis.residual_frozen_frame: H itself is frozen (its architecture and initial weights). The representation language (PyTorch tensors, the linear algebra substrate) is frozen. The ground truth test remains frozen. This is consistent with Stage 8 being the stage where 'The Ground Truth Is the Only Frozen Element'.

content.forward_viability_verdict: PASS (in-principle). ANIMA passes the Amendment 2 forward viability check. Stage 7 is NOT categorically impossible for this substrate, unlike the Living Seed. The mechanism path is clear: hypernetwork H within I generates I's own weight matrices. The substrate speaks the right language (tensors are data). The remaining work is engineering and empirical validation — not architectural impossibility.
critical_risks: c008 analog: hypernetwork self-modification can produce bang-bang oscillation if unbounded. T's cycle-based consolidation provides a natural damping point — modifications should only apply at cycle boundaries, not per-step.
Stage 7 exit criterion requires outperforming the unmodified version on NOVEL inputs, not just training tasks. This is the same overfitting trap that Stage 3 amendment required addressing.

The hypernetwork H adds frozen frame elements (H's own architecture). Amendment 1 applies: if H can be made adaptive without performance difference, it may be vacuously passed. But this is a Stage 8 concern, not Stage 7.
content.recommendation: Proceed with ANIMA substrate. Amendment 2 does not block stage progression. Stage 1 prototype should confirm ground truth compliance first (MI gap > 0 test, as assigned to Eli). Stage 7 path is validatable but not yet implemented.

constraints_implied: ANIMA Stage 7 requires hypernetwork H within I generating I's own update weights — do not attempt Stage 7 without this mechanism
Self-modification in ANIMA must apply at cycle boundaries (T consolidation points), not per-step — prevents c008-analog instability
Stage 7 exit criterion requires novel-input comparison, not training-input comparison — must test on held-out sequences
